title: Cloud Sandbox Sessions (Blaxel / Cloudflare / Vercel / Runloop)

blaxel_create: Caller → BlaxelSandboxClient: create(snapshot, manifest, options: BlaxelSandboxClientOptions)
BlaxelSandboxClient → BlaxelSDK: SandboxInstance.create_if_not_exists(create_config {name, image, memory, region, envs, ports, ttl})
blaxel_start: BlaxelSandboxSession → BlaxelSDK: sandbox.process.exec({command: 'mkdir -p {root}', wait_for_completion: true, timeout: 10000})
blaxel_ws_pty: BlaxelSandboxSession → _build_ws_url(sandbox_url, token, session_id, cwd, cols=80, rows=24)
blaxel_shutdown: Note: pause_on_exit=True keeps sandbox alive; Blaxel auto-resumes on next connection
blaxel_resume: Alt: BlaxelSandboxClient → BlaxelSDK: create_if_not_exists(config)  [if get() fails or pause_on_exit=False]
cloudflare_create: Caller → CloudflareSandboxClient: create(snapshot, manifest, options: CloudflareSandboxClientOptions {worker_url, api_key})
cloudflare_http_routing: HTTP session lazy-created: _session() creates aiohttp.ClientSession with Authorization header if api_key present
cloudflare_shutdown: Note: CloudflareSandboxSession has no explicit shutdown(); inherits BaseSandboxSession.shutdown() no-op
Cloudflare sandbox lifecycle is managed server-side (TTL/expiry)
vercel_create: Caller → VercelSandboxClient: create(snapshot, manifest, options: VercelSandboxClientOptions)
VercelSandboxClient: _resolve_manifest_root(manifest) → overrides root to '/vercel/sandbox' if default
VercelSandboxClient → VercelSandboxSession: from_state(VercelSandboxSessionState{sandbox_id='', project_id, team_id, timeout_ms, runtime, ...}, token)
VercelSandboxSession → VercelSDK: _create_sandbox_with_retry(source, ports, timeout, resources, runtime, token, project_id, team_id, interactive, env, network_policy)
vercel_resume: Alt reconnect path (sandbox_id present): AsyncSandbox.get(sandbox_id, token, project_id, team_id)
runloop_create: Caller → RunloopSandboxClient: create(snapshot, manifest, options: RunloopSandboxClientOptions)
RunloopSandboxClient: validate manifest root vs user_parameters home
RunloopSandboxClient → RunloopSDK: sdk.devbox.create(timeout=timeouts.create_s, blueprint_id/name, env_vars, name, user_parameters, launch_parameters, tunnel, gateways, mcp, metadata, secrets)
runloop_start: If _skip_start=True AND snapshot.restorable(): check fingerprint → restore snapshot or re-apply ephemeral manifest
runloop_resume: If status!='running': raise → fallback to recreate
runloop_exposed_port: If empty: devbox.net.enable_tunnel(auth_mode='open', http_keep_alive=True, wake_on_http=False) → retry get_tunnel_url()

key_relationships: All four clients extend BaseSandboxClient[OptionsT] and implement create/resume/delete/deserialize_session_state
All four sessions extend BaseSandboxSession and override start()/shutdown(); Cloudflare uses BaseSandboxSession.shutdown() no-op
BaseSandboxClient._wrap_session() wraps inner BaseSandboxSession into public SandboxSession with Instrumentation
CloudflareSandboxClient depends_on aiohttp (HTTP/WS to Cloudflare Worker at worker_url)
BlaxelSandboxSession uses _build_ws_url() to construct wss:// terminal URL
CloudflareSandboxSession uses _url(path) and _ws_pty_url() to build HTTP/WS paths
VercelSandboxSession lazily creates Vercel sandbox via _ensure_sandbox() on first operation
RunloopSandboxSession uses _ensure_tunnel_url(port) for exposed port resolution via devbox tunnels

path_normalization.blaxel: normalize_path() inherited from BaseSandboxSession; workspace root default '/home/user'
path_normalization.vercel: normalize_path() lexical PurePosixPath normalization against manifest.root; validates path does not escape root; default root '/vercel/sandbox'
path_normalization.runloop: PurePosixPath normalization relative to runloop_home (/home/user or /root); validates relative_to(home)

important_notes: Blaxel: PTY sessions use dedicated WS connections per process; _build_ws_url converts HTTPS sandbox URL to WSS; pause_on_exit skips delete on shutdown
Cloudflare: manifest root is FIXED to '/workspace'; sandbox_id comes from server via POST /v1/sandbox; no explicit shutdown (server-managed TTL)
Vercel: sandbox lazily created on first _ensure_sandbox() call; supports two workspace_persistence modes: 'tar' (default) and 'snapshot'; no PTY support
Runloop: devbox is the compute primitive; suspend/resume cycle for pause_on_exit; tunnel for exposed ports; _skip_start optimization skips full manifest replay on reconnect
All backends: resume() tries to reconnect before recreating; _skip_start flag enables fast-resume path that skips full manifest materialization
